items.desc:
Agenda for CSLO meeting: 
1) General update on OCHA work, CT work, 
2) Oracle update from the ERP board and plan forward
3) Discussion on how to proceed with the "repository of tech steps" w/ Moto
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc:
Outcome of our chat with Moto on "rep. tech. specs.". 
Plan forward: 
- Develop one-pager concept note on requirements of this repository (TO BE REVIEWED IN MAY IN ROME)
- Set up meeting with LATAM to review SMART and assess if it responds to our needs
- Check feasibility to embed SMART to the oracle systems or check if GRMS can be configured to address this need
- Develop proposal to be presented to tech. divisions to collect funding for this initiative (CSLO to support in developing the proposal) 
Governance: 
- CSLP to lead this initiative - project task force to be appointed at a later stage
- CSLO maintain oversight and PM tracking and support when needed
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc: Proposal to be in rome on Thursday and Friday of week of May 18th
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc: Arslen on CSLO support to OCHA: CSLP and CSLDL are member of the meeting chaired by OER. Need to see first how we manage Haiti then go to OER, show what we are doing w// Haiti and then use that as leverage to expand/motivate support to more COs. It's more relevant to partecipate in meetings that OER is having w/ COs instead of these alignments with CSLP and CSLDL. Haiti's OCHA project has not been approved yet.
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc:
Plan for CSLO Operating model: 
1) Draft of complete CSLO-OM ready to be reviewed by 17/04
2) Draft of CSLO-OM to be shared with CSL by 24/04
3) Prepare external-facing version of the CSLO-OM by 24/04
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc: Success metrics to start measuring - integrate them in the tracker: a) Real impact / change in operations and SC performance; b) COs are happy and request more CSLO support; c) Sustainable funding model for CSLO post-MVP; d) Initiate SC policy change; e) Approved KPI framework and baseline measured.
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc:
Ask Arslen on thursday if we could update our CLSO alignment cadence to a bi-weekly format - Tuesday (30 min) and Thursday

Decision: keep it as it is - switch to a bi-weekly format one OCHA work begins
items.author: Vincenzo

items.desc:
Agenda for Thursday's meeting with Arslen: 
- Collect any general updates on CAPEX, OCHA, etc.
- Align on dates & plan for next trip to Rome (18/05 to 21/05)
- Agree on new cadence of the CSLO alignment (Tuesday and Thursday for 30 min) 
- Discuss any updates related to the OCHA support plan (any updates from OER? from Haiti? what's the plan for the Haiti response?)
- Presentation of the working solution for the OCHA-programme control tower and suggested plan for introduction to OER, data collection/review/reporting, etc.
- Discuss with Arslen on how to proceed with the selection of pilot COs (we are delayed here - proposed list of COs to run by Arslen (Haiti, Afghanistan, Pakistan, Madagascar)
- Systems component/discussion with Oracle update

- Pending support from OER to set up meeting with Rodrigue to present the OCHA-CT mock-up, discuss viability of the solution, ownership, and practical next steps
- Pending support from CSL to set up technical discussion with CSI on the hosting of this solution
Both meeting should be held by 01/05 at the latest!
